div.h1: .poll Implementation: poll_wait Subscription + Readiness Mask Return

p.em: Call Sequence, Synchronization Logic, and Common Implementation Pitfalls

g.text: /* This is ONLY done when 'wait' is non-NULL */
/* Note: Stale reads are okay; write path must wake_up() */
- If `poll` sleeps on a mutex held by the `write` path...
- And the `write` path is waiting for space...
- Spurious wakeups (returning ready when not) are safe because
the subsequent `read()` will check the mutex and return `-EAGAIN`.

text.tspan: /* This is ONLY done when 'wait' is non-NULL */

- Spurious wakeups (returning ready when not) are safe because
the subsequent `read()` will check the mutex and return `-EAGAIN`.

g.text: return 0 (Logic error: still says not ready)

text.tspan: NO MUTEX used here to avoid
